generate.staleBanner: ⚠️ Settings changed — regenerate to update your bake plan
generate.emptyBakePlan: Complete your dough setup to generate your bake plan
generate.confirmOverwrite: This will overwrite your saved bake plan. Continue?

pizza.label: Pizza
pizza.desc: Neapolitan, New York, Roman, Detroit & Sourdough

bread.label: Bread
bread.desc: Pain au levain, Pain de campagne, Baguette & more

quantity.largeBatchNote: Large batch — consider splitting into two sessions

results.computeError: Could not compute recipe — please check your style selection and schedule times.
results.largeBatchDesc: Large dough mass retains heat longer — fermentation may be faster than calculated. Fine-tune if needed.

scheduler.bakeTimeLabelPizza: When does it go in the oven?
scheduler.bakeTimeLabelBread: When does it go in the oven?
scheduler.bakeTimeSub: Baker Hub builds your schedule backwards from here.
scheduler.startBeforeBake: Start time must be before bake time.
scheduler.startMovedNote: Start moved to {time} to avoid your unavailability block.

scenario.plentyPreferredSub: Starting a few hours later is still great — just a shorter cold rest.
scenario.plentySub: Anywhere from {start} to {end} gives good results.
scenario.tight: Tight window — start as soon as possible.
scenario.tooShort: Very little time — start now for the best you can get.

blockers.heading: Any time constraints?
blockers.sub: Optional — add your unavailable windows and we'll plan around them.
blockers.noOvernights: No overnight periods in this schedule.

hydration.noteStiff: Dough will be quite stiff — a little more water may help with stretching.
hydration.noteBelowClassic: Below the {name} classic range. Dough will be firmer and a bit denser.
hydration.noteClassic: Authentic {name} range. Great handling and traditional texture.
hydration.noteExtended: More open crumb and airiness. Requires confident shaping technique.
hydration.noteExtreme: Extreme hydration. Expect very sticky dough — wet hands, bench scraper essential.
hydration.noteHigh: High hydration territory. Excellent open crumb but challenging to handle.

oil.traditionalHighTemp: Traditional — no oil. Works beautifully at high-temp (450°C+).
oil.traditionalHome: Classic {style} uses no oil. Keeps the dough extensible and light.
oil.highTempNote: For best results in a high-temp oven, keeping oil at 0% works beautifully — oil can cause burning at very high temps.
oil.home1: Helps browning and tenderness at home oven temps. Classic choice for home baking.
oil.home2: Pan pizza range — creates a crispy, almost-fried base. Right at home in Detroit and pan styles.
oil.high: Entering enriched dough territory — high oil softens gluten development. An osmotolerant yeast (SAF Gold) helps.

sugar.traditionalHighTemp: Traditional — no sugar. Works beautifully at high-temp.
sugar.traditionalHome: Classic. Add 0.5% to help caramelisation in a home oven below 280°C.
sugar.subtle: Subtle colour boost. Good for home oven baking below 280°C.
sugar.noticeable: Noticeable sweetness and good browning. Works well for enriched styles.
sugar.osmotic: Above 2%, sugar adds osmotic stress on yeast — fermentation slows. Add extra 10% yeast.
sugar.high: High sugar territory (brioche level) — standard yeast may struggle. SAF Gold recommended.

simple.instant: Fine powder sachet. Most reliable, longest shelf life.
simple.active_dry: Brown granules. Widely available. Dissolve in warm water first.
simple.fresh: Soft beige block. Slightly richer flavour. Use within 2 weeks.
simple.sourdough: Wild fermentation. Deeper flavour, better digestibility. Needs an active starter.

custom.active_dry: Brown granules · ×1.33 vs IDY · proof in 38°C water
custom.fresh: Soft block · ×3.0 vs IDY · keep refrigerated
custom.sourdough: Wild yeast · replaces formula · needs active starter at peak

avpn.limitDesc: 280g max per ball — the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana standard for authentic Neapolitan pizza.
avpn.body: The AVPN defines the official standard for authentic Neapolitan pizza. Maximum ball weight: 280g. Diameter: 22–35cm. This ensures the proper ratio of crust to topping and the characteristic light, airy cornicione.

climate.cityNotFound: City not found. Try a different spelling or nearby city.
climate.warmNudge: In a warm climate, afternoon temps can push above 28°C. If your kitchen heats up during the day, consider entering your expected peak temperature instead.
climate.fridgeWarning: Fridge at {temp}°C is warmer than the standard 4°C — yeast will be more active during cold retard.

timeline.autoAdjusted: Schedule auto-adjusted — a room temperature window was too long for your kitchen temperature. A cold retard phase was added automatically to prevent over-fermentation.

steps.restRT: Remove from fridge — rest at room temperature

prefSteps.tipPoolishFridge: Mix equal weight flour and water (100% hydration) with a pinch of yeast. Stir until combined. Cover tightly and place in the fridge.
prefSteps.tipPoolishRT: Mix equal weight flour and water (100% hydration) with a pinch of yeast. Stir until no dry flour remains. Leave at room temperature.
prefSteps.tipBiga: Combine flour, water and a tiny amount of yeast (0.1–0.2%). Mix roughly — biga is intentionally shaggy, not smooth. Cover and refrigerate.
prefSteps.tipRemoveBiga: Take your biga out of the fridge — no warmup needed, biga goes straight into the mix cold.
prefSteps.tipRemovePoolishShort: Take your poolish out of the fridge now — time is short before mixing. Watch for peak rather than the clock.
prefSteps.tipFeedHot: Feed equal weights starter + flour + water. At {temp} your starter peaks in 3–5h — watch for dome + bubbles.
prefSteps.tipFeedWarm: Feed equal weights starter + flour + water. Peaks in 4–6h at {temp}.
prefSteps.tipFeedRT: Feed equal weights starter + flour + water. Peaks in 6–10h at room temperature.

mixTips.sourdough: Combine flour and water, then add your starter. Add salt with the remaining water. Mix until fully absorbed.
mixTips.hand: Flour + 90% water first — cover and rest 20 min (autolyse). Then yeast, salt, remaining water, and knead 8–12 min.
mixTips.spiral: Flour + water + yeast at Speed 1. Add salt, then Speed 2 until pumpkin shape forms and the windowpane test passes.
mixTips.noKnead: Combine all ingredients and mix until no dry flour remains — time and stretch & folds do the rest.
mixTips.stand: Flour + water at Speed 1. Add yeast, then salt, then remaining water. Speed 2 until dough clears the bowl and passes the windowpane test.

bulkTips.long: Cover tightly and place in a warm spot away from drafts. Perform 4 sets of stretch & folds in the first 2 hours.
bulkTips.medium: Cover tightly. Perform 2–3 sets of stretch & folds every 20–30 minutes.
bulkTips.short: Cover tightly. Perform one set of stretch & folds after 15 minutes, then cover and rest.
bulkTips.veryShort: Cover tightly and rest — dough goes straight to the fridge after this short window.

coldTips.bulk: Whole dough mass goes into the fridge. Cold bulk fermentation slows yeast activity and develops flavour. No action needed.
coldTips.balls: Individual dough balls rest in the fridge. This firms them up and makes final proofing more controlled.
coldTips.single: Dough rests in the fridge. Cold fermentation builds flavour slowly — no action needed, time works for you.

timeline.divideTip: Weigh and divide dough into {n} ball{plural}. Pinch the bottom tight for surface tension.
timeline.divideTipCold: Cold dough is easier to ball — work quickly before it warms up.
timeline.divideTipWarm: ⚠️ Your kitchen is warm — get balls back in the fridge within 20 minutes.

finalProofTips.withCold: Remove balls from fridge and rest until slightly soft, then proof covered at room temperature. Start preheating your oven during the final proof.
finalProofTips.withoutCold: Shape dough balls and proof covered at room temperature. Start preheating your oven during the final proof.

preheatTips.long: Start now — while dough finishes its final proof. Give it the full {min} min to fully saturate your baking surface.
preheatTips.short: Start now — while dough finishes its final proof. Set oven to maximum temperature for {min} minutes.

timeline.eatTip: Your dough is perfectly fermented. Score, load, and bake. Buon appetito!
timeline.restRtTip: Take dough balls out of the fridge and leave covered at room temperature. Cold dough is too stiff to stretch — wait until slightly soft.
timeline.finalProofCovered: Balls are already shaped — cover and leave at room temperature until the poke test.
timeline.finalProofShape: Shape your balls if not done. Cover and leave at room temperature until the poke test.

recipeOutput.largeBatchFinalDough: Your final dough is ~{grams}g — more than your {mixer} can handle at once. Split into {n} batches.
recipeOutput.largeBatchTotal: Your total dough is {grams}g — more than your {mixer} can handle at once. Split into {n} batches.
recipeOutput.precisionScaleBody: Your yeast amount is {amount} — too small for a standard kitchen scale. Use a precision scale accurate to 0.1g, or use the dilution method.
recipeOutput.dilutionTitle: Dilution required — amount too small to weigh directly
recipeOutput.poolishBody: Long room-temperature fermentation is hard to control. A poolish gives you better flavour and more predictable timing. Mix 50% of your flour with an equal weight of water and a pinch of yeast 8–16h before baking.
recipeOutput.notRecommendedTitle: This fermentation schedule is not recommended
recipeOutput.notRecommendedBody: The combination of time and temperature will likely cause over-fermentation. Add a cold retard phase or reduce your start time.
recipeOutput.flourTooltipBread: Strong bread flour works best — T65 or W200+. Plain flour works for short ferments.
recipeOutput.flourTooltipPizza: Italian 00 or T45 forte gives the best results. Using plain flour or T55? Switch to Custom mode to adapt your recipe.

starterReady.floatTest: Float test: drop a piece in water — floats ✓

schedulePicker.guardPast: This bake time has passed — try picking a later date.
schedulePicker.guardShort: Working with a short window — same-day dough can still be wonderful.
schedulePicker.blockerNote: Start Dough falls in one of your busy windows — that's fine if it works for you.
schedulePicker.bulkNote: Your dough needs ~{dur} to rise — be free by {time} to put it in the fridge, or start a bit earlier.
schedulePicker.fallbackBtn1: Start just before or after my busy time
schedulePicker.fallbackBtn2: Mix during my busy time — I'll make it work
schedulePicker.fallbackBtn2Sub: Still within the ideal window — dough will be excellent.
schedulePicker.gotIt: Got it, I'll make it work

flourNote.short: Pizza or bread flour works well for this plan.
flourNote.medium: Pizza flour gives the best results — Italian 00 is ideal. French T45 forte works too 🌾
flourNote.long: Strong pizza flour recommended — Italian 00 W270+. French alternative: T45 forte W260+ 🌾
flourNote.adapt: Using plain flour or T55? Tap to adapt →
flourNote.breadShort: Bread flour works well for this plan.
flourNote.breadMedium: Strong bread flour recommended — T65 or bread flour W200+ 🌾
flourNote.breadLong: High-protein flour essential — T65 forte or bread flour W260+ 🌾

recipe.yeastReassurance: Yes, that's intentional — less yeast, more time = deeper flavour. Trust the process. 🍕
recipe.precisionScale: Precision scale recommended — this is a very small amount of yeast.
